name: get_agent_run_trace
description: Get the trace for a single Agent Run from an eve agent, including turns, messages, reasoning, tool calls, token usage, and tool input/output when available. Use this for debugging exact agent behavior in production.

teamId.description:
The team ID to get the deployment events for. Alternatively the team slug can be used.
Team IDs start with "team_".
If you do not know the team ID or slug, it can be found through these mechanism:
- Read the file .vercel/project.json if it exists and extract the orgId
- Use the `list_teams` tool

projectId.description:
The project ID to get the deployment events for. Alternatively the project slug can be used.
Project IDs start with "prj_".
If you do not know the project ID or slug, it can be found through these mechanism:
- Read the file .vercel/project.json if it exists and extract the projectId
- Use the `list_projects` tool

runId.description: The Agent Run ID to inspect.

environment.description: Agent run environment, usually "production" or "preview". Defaults to "production".

period.description: Preset time range. Ignored when both from and to are provided. Defaults to the dashboard endpoint default.

from.description: Start time as ISO 8601, Unix seconds, Unix milliseconds, or a relative duration like "12h". Must be used with to.

to.description: End time as ISO 8601, Unix seconds, Unix milliseconds, a relative duration like "1h", or "now". Must be used with from.

maxFieldLength.description: Maximum length for individual string fields in the returned trace. Defaults to 8000; use 0 to disable truncation.
